messages.content: paraphrase "many experts advice against using it"
"Numerous experts strongly discourage its usage."
paraphrase "Here's a recent one from TechCrunch, which is intended to show how much they cover start-ups versus big companies."
"Presented here is a recent example from TechCrunch that highlights their extensive coverage of start-ups in comparison to established corporations."
paraphrase "does not represent anything"
"Doesn't signify or stand for anything."
paraphrase “does not represent anything, it is simply used as categorical differentiator"
"It lacks any symbolic meaning; instead, it serves solely as a categorical differentiator."
paraphrase "My strong opinion is that color should always be an explicit choice and should be used strategically to draw the audience's eye. This preattentive power is being wasted here. "

"I firmly believe that color should be a deliberate decision, used strategically to attract the attention of the audience. However, this inherent ability to capture attention is being squandered in this context."
paraphrase "if the data items have small differences and the magnitude comparisons are importan"
"If there are minor variations between data items and comparing their magnitudes is crucial..."
paraphrase "Also, angles with horizontal bisectors (when the line dividing the angle in two is horizontal) appear larger than angles with vertical bisectors"
"In addition, angles having horizontal bisectors, where the line dividing the angle horizontally, appear larger compared to angles with vertical bisectors."
paraphrase “Also, angles with horizontal bisectors appear larger than angles with vertical bisectors”
"Furthermore, angles that have horizontal bisectors appear to be larger in size when compared to angles with vertical bisectors."

proofreading "It has been found that pie charts can be read easily and accurately if the slice size close to quarter, half or full. That because the angle of a slice where the actual angle less than 90 degrees could be underestimate it or can be overestimated if it was greater than that. Additionally, angles that have horizontal bisectors seems to be larger than angles with vertical bisectors."

"It has been found that pie charts can be easily and accurately read when the slice size approximates a quarter, half, or full of the chart. This is because when the angle of a slice is less than 90 degrees, it can be underestimated, or overestimated if it exceeds 90 degrees. Furthermore, angles with horizontal bisectors tend to appear larger than angles with vertical bisectors."

paraphrasing "if the slices are different across the pies, the pieces shift around; this plus the spatial separation and lack of alignment to a common baseline make comparing slices across pies difficult. "

"When the slices vary between pies, the individual pieces shift position, making it challenging to compare slices across different pies. Additionally, the spatial separation and absence of alignment to a shared baseline further complicate the comparison of slices between pies."

proofreading "Furthermore, pie charts should display percents sum to 100% to produce a meaningful whole. That could not achieved in some cases like in a survey when allowed people to select more than one answers. As the following example (figure 3, from [2]) the percentage is up to more than 100%."

"Furthermore, it is essential for pie charts to display percentages that add up to 100% in order to present a coherent and meaningful whole. However, this cannot be achieved in certain cases, such as surveys that allow people to select multiple answers. In the following example (Figure 3, from [2]), the percentages add up to more than 100%."
